finding.description: The search method uses raw SQL queries, which can lead to SQL injection vulnerabilities.
finding.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-01] Raw SQL query — SQL injection vulnerability
finding.recommendation: Use Eloquent ORM methods to prevent SQL injection.
tests: Verify that the search method is safe from SQL injection.

finding.description: The Vendor model uses $fillable instead of $guarded, violating the convention for mass assignment protection.
finding.recommendation: Change the model to use $guarded = ['id']; instead of $fillable.
tests: Test that mass assignment works correctly with the guarded property.
finding.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-02 for mass assignment conventions.

finding.description: The search view uses {!! !!} to output vendor names, which can lead to XSS vulnerabilities if not properly sanitized.
finding.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-03] XSS vulnerability in search view
finding.recommendation: Change {!! $vendor->name !!} to {{ $vendor->name }} to ensure proper escaping.
tests: Test that the output is properly escaped to prevent XSS.
finding.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-03 for XSS prevention.

finding.description: The store method does not validate the request data.
finding.recommendation: Add $request->validate([...]) to ensure data integrity in the store method.
tests: Test that invalid data is rejected in the store method.

finding.description: The Vendor model is missing the HasCreator trait, which is required for tracking the creator of the model.
finding.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-07] Missing HasCreator trait in Vendor model
finding.recommendation: Add the HasCreator trait to the Vendor model.
tests: Test that the creator of the vendor is tracked correctly.
finding.notes:
Refer to rule CTDL-07 for audit tracking.
Severity normalized to MAJOR based on control risk floor.

finding.description: The Vendor model is missing the explicit definition of the $table property with the escm_ prefix.
finding.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-10] Missing $table property in Vendor model
finding.recommendation: Add the $table property to the Vendor model: protected $table = 'escm_vendors';
tests: Test that the model correctly references the escm_vendors table.
finding.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-10 for table naming conventions.

finding.description: The index method does not call checkAccessPermission() and lacks scopeProject() for multi-tenant isolation.
finding.recommendation: Add checkAccessPermission() and ESCM::scopeProject(auth()->user()) to the index method.
tests: Verify that access permissions are enforced for the index method.

finding.description: The store method logs sensitive data without masking.
finding.check_name: SAST | [WIKA-Q08] Logging sensitive data without masking
finding.recommendation: Mask sensitive fields like password and pin before logging.
tests: Ensure sensitive data is not logged in plain text.

fp_findings.description: The store method does not validate the incoming request data, which can lead to invalid data being saved to the database.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [WIKA-Q02] Missing validation in store method
fp_findings.recommendation: Add validation to the store method using $request->validate([...]) to ensure data integrity.
tests: Test that invalid data returns a validation error.
Test that valid data is saved correctly.
fp_findings.notes: Refer to rule WIKA-Q02 for validation requirements.

fp_findings.description: The search method uses a raw SQL query, which violates the convention of using Eloquent ORM for database access.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-01] Raw SQL query in search method
fp_findings.recommendation: Replace the raw SQL query with an Eloquent query using the Vendor model.
tests: Test that the search functionality works with Eloquent queries.
Test for SQL injection vulnerabilities.
fp_findings.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-01 for ORM usage.

fp_findings.description: The export method does not check for user permissions before exporting vendor data, which can lead to unauthorized access.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-11] Missing permission check in export method
fp_findings.recommendation: Add a permission check using checkAccessPermission() at the start of the export method.
tests: Test that unauthorized users cannot access the export functionality.
fp_findings.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-11 for authorization checks.

fp_findings.description: The Vendor model is missing the ActivityLogged trait, which is required for logging changes to the model.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-08] Missing ActivityLogged trait in Vendor model
fp_findings.recommendation: Add the ActivityLogged trait to the Vendor model.
tests: Test that changes to the vendor are logged correctly.
fp_findings.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-08 for audit tracking.

fp_findings.description: The detail method does not follow the Citadel page lifecycle.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-06] Does not follow Citadel Page lifecycle
fp_findings.recommendation: Refactor the detail method to follow the Citadel page lifecycle.
tests: Ensure the detail method adheres to the Citadel lifecycle.

fp_findings.description: The syncFromSap method does not extend the Base sync service.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [WIKA-Q06] Sync service does not extend Base
fp_findings.recommendation: Refactor the syncFromSap method to extend App ase sync service.
tests: Ensure the sync service follows the correct structure.

fp_findings.description: The callback method is vulnerable to open redirects.
fp_findings.recommendation: Validate the redirect URL to prevent open redirects.
tests: Test that the callback method does not allow open redirects.

fp_findings.description: The generateToken method contains hardcoded credentials.
fp_findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded credentials and use environment variables instead.
tests: Ensure no hardcoded credentials are present in the code.

fp_findings.description: The PurchaseOrder model uses $fillable instead of $guarded.
fp_findings.recommendation: Change $fillable to $guarded = ['id'] in the PurchaseOrder model.
tests: Verify that mass assignment is properly protected.
